project_structure: Monorepo with three packages: packages/cli (citty-based commands in src/commands/{install,uninstall,doctor,serve}.ts), packages/server (services consumed by CLI: serve-lock, doctor), packages/shared (i18n locale tables in src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN}.ts that the CLI's t() resolves at runtime). All flags flow through citty's args:{} block → InitArgs/UninstallArgs/DoctorArgs typed shape → resolveXxxCliIntent reducers → Options DTOs (InitOptions/UninstallOptions) → buildXxxExecutionPlan → executeXxxExecutionPlan. Tests live in packages/cli/__tests__/{*.test.ts, integration/*.test.ts} and assert on both the public command surface (cli-surface.test.ts snapshot) AND the plan/options shape (install-cli-surface, uninstall, doctor, serve-lock, init-guard, install-diff-mode).

relevant_files.rationale: Citty install args:{} block at L294-L353 declares all 12 flags being killed/renamed (force, plan→dry-run, reapply, bootstrap, mcp, hooks, interactive, mcp-install, scope) plus their downstream wiring in resolveInitCliIntent at L535-L559 which maps args.bootstrap/mcp/hooks to skipBootstrap/skipMcp/skipHooks and reads args['mcp-install']/args.scope. Force/reapply have deeper consumers at L372 (checkLockOrThrow), L389 (compat warning), L617-L625 (deprecation warnings), L665-L688 (drift-abort gates), L721-L728 (canonical-no-op confirmation), L806 (isReapply), L924 (scan re-run gate), L1093 (banner), L1551-L1576 (mode banners).

key_code.description: 12 flag definitions; rc.15 keeps target/debug/yes and renames plan→dry-run, kills force/reapply/bootstrap/mcp/hooks/interactive/mcp-install/scope. negativeDescription t() keys point at cli.install.args.no-bootstrap/no-mcp/no-hooks which are also slated for i18n removal.

Reducer that maps citty args → InitOptions DTO. Every consumer of force/reapply/skipBootstrap/skipMcp/skipHooks/mcp-install/scope flows through here; killing the flags means deleting the corresponding mappings AND the InitArgs / InitOptions type members at L37-L62.

Currently: lock is checked when args.reapply===true OR fabric is already initialized; --force bypasses. After rc.15, args.reapply disappears (always-checked-on-init'd-workspace stays), and there is no --force to thread, so the call becomes checkLockOrThrow(intent.target) with no opts.

Two consecutive guards bypassed by !plan.options.force && !plan.options.reapply. After kill, these become unconditional — drift always aborts (no legacy escape hatch). The drift-abort message currently mentions both `fab doctor` (inspect) and `fab uninstall && fab install` (reset) per rc.14 — this stays unchanged.
cli.install.diff.deprecation-force / -reapply printed when these flags are still passed in rc.14. In rc.15 these whole blocks are deleted AND the i18n keys are removed.

Prints cli.install.compat.{plan,interactive,legacy-stage-flags}. The legacy-stage-flags and interactive notices die with the flag removals; the plan compat message likely renames to dry-run (or is dropped since --dry-run becomes the canonical form).

relevant_files.topic_relation: Direct surface-removal target for rc.15 Phase 2 cleanup — install.ts owns 8 of the 12 flags being killed/renamed plus three nested consumer paths (lock-gate, drift-gate, deprecation-warning). Every kill ripples through citty args → InitArgs type → InitOptions type → resolveInitCliIntent → buildInitExecutionPlan → executeInitExecutionPlan.

relevant_files.rationale: Citty uninstall args:{} block at L185-L240 declares 11 flags including the 7 being killed (force, interactive, bootstrap, mcp, scaffold, purge, clean-empties) and the rename target (plan→dry-run). The reducer resolveUninstallCliIntent at L307-L327 maps each flag to UninstallOptions. Critical: args.force gates checkLockOrThrow at L259 AND the destructive-confirm bypass at L294; args['clean-empties'] threads through to executeUninstallExecutionPlan at L646 where it controls BootstrapUninstallOptions.cleanEmpties.

key_code.description: 11 flag definitions; rc.15 keeps target/debug/yes, renames plan→dry-run, kills force/interactive/bootstrap/mcp/scaffold/purge/clean-empties. The --clean-empties flag flips from opt-in to default behavior.

Currently reads args['clean-empties'] === true. After the flag is killed AND the behavior becomes default, this line becomes cleanEmpties: true (or the flag is dropped from UninstallOptions entirely and the consumer at L646 is hardcoded to {cleanEmpties: true}).

Two consumers of args.force: (1) lock check passes {force: args.force} to checkLockOrThrow — after rc.15 this becomes checkLockOrThrow(intent.target) with no opts, lock conflicts always abort. (2) Confirm prompt bypass `args.yes !== true && args.force !== true` — after kill becomes `args.yes !== true`.

Wizard asks 'Cascade-remove empty arrays/objects in hook configs?' with initialValue from context.options.cleanEmpties. If clean-empties becomes default-on, this prompt can be removed entirely (wizard never asks) OR the prompt stays but defaults to true. Need clarification.

`const opts: BootstrapUninstallOptions = { cleanEmpties: plan.options.cleanEmpties === true }`. If the option survives but defaults to true at the resolveUninstallCliIntent boundary, this line stays. If the whole option type is dropped (cleanEmpties is implicit in uninstall-skills-and-hooks), this and the prop in UninstallOptions both disappear.

skipBootstrap / skipMcp / skipScaffold are passed through to buildUninstallExecutionPlan stage gates. Killing --bootstrap/--mcp/--scaffold means the stage-skipping ability disappears: every uninstall always runs all three stages. The 'lockedStages' wizard path at L829 also collapses.

Killing --purge means: (a) the buildUninstallFabricPlan no longer adds knowledge-subdir entries (preservation becomes unconditional), (b) the wizard's purge prompt disappears (uninstall always preserves .fabric/knowledge/). Existing safe-guard against ~/.fabric/knowledge/ remains. Test (d) in uninstall.test.ts must be deleted or rewritten.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Direct surface-removal target — uninstall.ts owns the most subtle change: --clean-empties default flip (currently opt-in, becomes always-on). Killing --purge means knowledge preservation is unconditional; killing --interactive means wizard auto-detect via terminalInteractive; killing --bootstrap/--mcp/--scaffold removes per-stage opt-out (full-run only).

relevant_files.rationale: Citty doctor args:{} block at L64-L102 declares 7 flags including --force at L84-L88 (kills) and --apply-lint at L89-L93 (renames to --fix-knowledge). The --rescan flag does NOT yet exist and must be added — likely shelling into runInitScan from scan.ts (line 178 in scan.ts) the same way install.ts:926 does. Lock-bypass via {force: args.force} at L108 needs to become unconditional lock-or-abort.

key_code.description: 7 flag definitions; rc.15 keeps target/fix/json/strict/yes, kills force, renames apply-lint → fix-knowledge, adds rescan. The --fix-knowledge rename ripples to: applyLint variable at L110, applyLintReport at L123/L156/L168/L184/L202, computeApplyLintPlan() at L307, renderApplyLintPlan/renderApplyLintMutations, plus the entire safety-confirm flow at L127-L156.

Currently passes {force: args.force} to bypass lock. After rc.15 becomes checkLockOrThrow(resolution.target) with no opts — doctor refuses to run while serve holds the lock. NEEDS USER DECISION on what message: silent abort vs. 'fab serve is running (PID N) — stop it first' guidance message.

Outside the citty block, every `apply-lint` / `applyLint` identifier needs renaming (note: variable names are internal — can stay as `applyLint` OR rename to `fixKnowledge`; the spec only requires the CLI flag rename). The DoctorApplyLintReport import from @fenglimg/fabric-server, runDoctorApplyLint, and ApplyLintPlan type names also belong to the server package — NOT touched by CLI surface contraction (they're internal contract names).

New boolean flag. Implementation: when args.rescan === true, call await runInitScan(resolution.target, { source: 'doctor-rescan' }) before report. Mirrors install.ts:926 — runInitScan rebuilds .fabric/agents.meta.json + per-file forensic state. Should run BEFORE the report so the report reflects the freshly-scanned state.

Currently writes cli.doctor.errors.apply-lint-fix-mutually-exclusive. After rename, the i18n key becomes cli.doctor.errors.fix-knowledge-fix-mutually-exclusive (or similar). If --rescan is added, decide whether --rescan + --fix-knowledge are mutually exclusive (probably no — rescan is read-only, can compose with anything).

relevant_files.topic_relation: Direct surface-removal target — doctor.ts has THREE distinct rc.15 changes (kill --force, rename --apply-lint → --fix-knowledge, add --rescan). The --force kill is the highest-risk because it removes the only escape hatch when a stale serve lock is misdetected; users will have to either kill the serve PID or wait for lock expiry.

relevant_files.rationale: Citty serve args:{} block at L24-L48 declares 5 flags; --force at L44-L48 is killed in rc.15. Currently passed to acquireLock at L61 as {force: args.force}, which bypasses ServeLockHeldError when another live serve PID holds the lock. After kill, acquireLock(projectRoot) becomes a strict assertion — duplicate serve attempts always abort.

key_code.description: 5 flag definitions: port/host/target/debug/force. rc.15 kills force; remaining 4 are untouched.

`acquireLock(projectRoot, { force: args.force })` becomes `acquireLock(projectRoot)` after kill. The server-side AcquireOptions.force parameter survives because serve-lock.test.ts uses it in unit tests — the CLI just stops threading it through.
Drop the `force?: boolean` member. Note: this is a TypeScript type, not a runtime contract — no migration needed beyond the removal.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Direct surface-removal target — smallest surface (single flag). Behavioral effect: once another live serve holds the lock, the new serve attempt always throws ServeLockHeldError. The action hint in serve-lock.ts:51 currently says 'or run with --force to override' — this message must be updated to point at killing the other PID instead.

relevant_files.rationale: i18n description keys for ALL the CLI flag args live here and must be deleted in lock-step with the citty args removal. Direct keys to remove: cli.install.args.{force, reapply, no-bootstrap, no-mcp, no-hooks, interactive}.description, cli.install.mcp.{install.prompt, install.invalid, install.global, install.local, scope.description, scope.invalid, scope.project, scope.user}, cli.install.force.{overwritten, warning}, cli.install.diff.deprecation-{force, reapply}, cli.install.compat.{interactive, legacy-stage-flags}, cli.install.plan.mode-banner.{reapply, plan-reapply}, cli.install.mode.{reapply, badge.reapply, badge.plan-reapply}, cli.uninstall.args.force.description, cli.uninstall.flags.{no-bootstrap, no-mcp, no-scaffold, interactive, purge, clean-empties}, cli.uninstall.wizard.clean-empties, cli.doctor.args.force.description, cli.doctor.args.apply-lint.description (rename to fix-knowledge.description), cli.serve.args.force.description.

Drop force/no-*/interactive/purge/clean-empties; rename plan→dry-run. Note: cli.uninstall.plan.summary at L243 includes purge={purge} clean-empties={cleanEmpties} placeholder fields — string template must be updated.

Drop cli.serve.args.force.description. The cli.serve.error.port-in-use at L? remains (separate concern).

Missing this step leaves orphaned t() keys that lint passes will flag (knip / lint-protected-tokens). The translation pairs MUST stay in lock-step (no en-only or zh-only deletes).

After rc.15 default flip, every call site passes cleanEmpties:true (default) and the option can be either (a) retained as internal API surface for direct callers OR (b) dropped from the option type entirely with cleanEmpties hardcoded throughout.

After the CLI flag is killed AND default flips to always-clean, this can either survive as an internal toggle (with cleanEmpties: true default in the type) or be deleted entirely — depends on whether anyone other than the CLI calls these helpers with cleanEmpties:false.
`if (!cleanEmpties || filtered.length > 0) {...}`. If the option is deleted, this whole guard simplifies to unconditional cascade-prune.

After default flip, all current `cleanEmpties:true` tests describe the DEFAULT behavior; any cleanEmpties:false tests (if any) describe a path that no longer exists.

relevant_files.rationale: runInitScan at L178 is the rescan implementation called by install.ts:926 — doctor's new --rescan flag will call this same function. No modifications needed; this file is a pattern_reference for how --rescan should invoke the scan engine.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Reference for the new --rescan flag implementation. Doctor's rescan handler invokes runInitScan(resolution.target, { source: 'doctor-rescan' }) — mirror of install.ts:926's pattern.

relevant_files.rationale: AcquireOptions.force at L19-L21 survives — it's an engine-level parameter that the server's own tests and any future internal-API caller can use. Only the CLI stops threading args.force through. The ServeLockHeldError action hint at L51-L52 mentions '--force to override' which must be updated since the CLI flag is gone.

key_code.description: Two strings: 'Stop the other process (PID {N}) or run with --force to override'. Update both to drop the '--force' hint — users must kill the other PID.

relevant_files.topic_relation: Message-only update — the engine API surface is unchanged, but the user-facing error guidance currently mentions a flag that no longer exists.

patterns:
**Citty args removal pattern**: Delete the entry from the args:{} object → delete the corresponding optional field from the typed Args shape (InitArgs / UninstallArgs / DoctorArgs / ServeArgs) → delete consumer reads of args.<flag> (both `args.<flag>` member access AND `args['<kebab-case>']` index access) → delete consumer reads of options.<derived-field> (e.g. options.reapply, options.force, options.cleanEmpties) → delete the field from the options DTO type (InitOptions / UninstallOptions) → delete the field from the builder function input type (buildInitExecutionPlan, buildUninstallExecutionPlan) → delete dependent gates / banners / mode-specific code paths.

**i18n cleanup pattern**: For each killed flag, delete the description key from BOTH packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts AND zh-CN.ts (key parity is enforced). Mode-specific banners (cli.install.plan.mode-banner.reapply, mode.badge.reapply, plan-reapply variants) and deprecation messages (cli.install.diff.deprecation-{force,reapply}) and compat messages (cli.install.compat.{interactive,legacy-stage-flags}) also need deletion.

**Rename pattern (--plan → --dry-run)**: Rename the citty key in args:{} → update consumer reads (args.plan → args['dry-run']) → update the internal field name if desired (planOnly is internal — can stay or rename) → rename i18n keys (cli.install.args.plan.description → cli.install.args.dry-run.description, cli.install.compat.plan → drop or rename, cli.install.plan.mode-banner.* → cli.install.mode-banner.*) → regenerate cli-surface.test.ts.snap.

**Rename pattern (--apply-lint → --fix-knowledge)**: Same as --plan rename. Internal variable names (applyLint, applyLintReport, applyLintSpy, ApplyLintPlan type, runDoctorApplyLint, DoctorApplyLintReport) belong to the server package's internal contract — they can either be renamed in lock-step OR left as the internal naming with only the CLI flag renamed.

**Default-flip pattern (--clean-empties)**: (a) Delete the citty flag → (b) at resolveUninstallCliIntent, replace `args['clean-empties'] === true` with `true` (or delete the field entirely from UninstallOptions and hardcode at the call site) → (c) decide: keep cleanEmpties as an internal option to BootstrapUninstallOptions (allows future opt-out) OR delete the option entirely throughout uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts → (d) delete the wizard's clean-empties prompt at uninstall.ts:766-771 since there's no user-facing toggle → (e) delete the i18n keys.

**ADD pattern (--rescan on doctor)**: Add citty entry `rescan: { type: 'boolean', description: t('cli.doctor.args.rescan.description'), default: false }` → import runInitScan from ../commands/scan.js (or invoke via the server package if exposed there) → in doctor's run() function, after the preflight checkLockOrThrow but before runDoctorReport: `if (args.rescan === true) await runInitScan(resolution.target, { source: 'doctor-rescan' });` → add i18n keys to en.ts and zh-CN.ts → add unit test that mocks runInitScan and asserts it was called when args.rescan:true.
dependencies:
**Internal**:
- packages/cli/src/commands/{install,uninstall,doctor,serve}.ts → packages/shared/src/i18n via t() function for every t('cli.*.args.*.description') string
- packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts → packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts (runInitScan, detectExistingLanguage)
- packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts → @fenglimg/fabric-server (checkLockOrThrow)
- packages/cli/src/commands/uninstall.ts → @fenglimg/fabric-server (checkLockOrThrow)
- packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts → @fenglimg/fabric-server (checkLockOrThrow, runDoctorReport, runDoctorFix, runDoctorApplyLint)
- packages/cli/src/commands/serve.ts → @fenglimg/fabric-server (acquireLock, releaseLock, startHttpServer)
- packages/cli/src/commands/uninstall.ts → packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts (cleanEmpties threading)

**External**:
- citty (defineCommand, args:{} schema)
- @clack/prompts (confirm/select for wizards — orthogonal to flags)
- vitest (test framework)

**Implicit (runtime string lookup)**:
- t('cli.<command>.args.<flag>.description') is resolved at command parse time from the i18n locale map. If the citty arg is killed but the i18n key remains, the orphaned key is harmless but flagged by knip / lint-protected-tokens. If the citty arg survives but the i18n key is killed, t() returns undefined and the help output shows the literal key path — a noisier failure mode.
integration_points:
**citty args:{} blocks** (4 commands × ~5-12 flags each) — the public CLI surface. Tests at packages/cli/__tests__/cli-surface.test.ts snapshot this surface for regression detection.

**The t() i18n boundary** (packages/shared/src/i18n) — every flag has a description key. The i18n.test.ts at packages/cli/__tests__/ enforces en/zh-CN parity; orphan keys after the cleanup will fail lint.

**The Args type → Options DTO reducer** (resolveInitCliIntent / resolveUninstallCliIntent at install.ts:535 and uninstall.ts:307) — this is the single funnel where every flag becomes a structured option. Reducer-level deletion is sufficient; downstream code reads from the Options DTO, not from raw args.

**Lock-check API contract** (@fenglimg/fabric-server's checkLockOrThrow and acquireLock with optional {force?:boolean}) — the engine-level escape hatch survives even after the CLI flags are killed. CLI just stops passing the option through; internal/server tests that pass {force:true} continue to work.

**Wizard adapters** (createDefaultInitWizardAdapter, createDefaultUninstallWizardAdapter) — these consume Options DTO fields like skipBootstrap/skipMcp/skipHooks and cleanEmpties to set initial wizard values. After flag kills, the wizard always asks for these stages from scratch (no CLI override). For --clean-empties default flip, the wizard's clean-empties prompt becomes redundant.

**Snapshot test** (cli-surface.test.ts.snap) — regenerate after every flag change; the diff is the proof of the surface contraction.
constraints:
**1. citty kebab-case auto-translation**: citty translates `args: { 'dry-run': ... }` to consumer access `args['dry-run']` (not `args.dryRun`). The --plan → --dry-run rename will force every test to update `args.plan` → `args['dry-run']`. (Verify against citty docs / existing 'mcp-install' / 'apply-lint' / 'clean-empties' patterns which all use bracket access.)

**2. i18n locale parity**: en.ts and zh-CN.ts MUST have identical key sets — i18n.test.ts enforces this. Every kill in en.ts requires a paired kill in zh-CN.ts.

**3. Server-side API parameters survive**: @fenglimg/fabric-server's AcquireOptions.force is used by serve-lock.test.ts unit tests AND is documented as a runtime escape hatch for internal callers. Do NOT delete the parameter from the server package; only stop threading args.force through from the CLI.

**4. Drift-abort message references --force / fab uninstall**: cli.install.diff.drift-abort currently mentions both bypass paths. After rc.15, the message must drop the --force reference and only mention `fab uninstall && fab install` reset.

**5. ServeLockHeldError action hint mentions --force**: serve-lock.ts:51 and L104 emit 'or run with --force to override' as the actionHint. This message is consumed by user-facing error renderers — update to drop --force.

**6. clean-empties default-flip is irreversible from CLI**: Once --clean-empties is killed AND default flips to always-on, users who actively wanted empty arrays preserved (if any) have no flag-level workaround. Need to verify no current users depend on the opt-in behavior (likely safe — empty arrays in hook configs are noise).

**7. --reapply was the documented drift-recovery flag in rc.14**: rc.14's deprecation message says --reapply will be removed in rc.15. Tests at init-guard.test.ts:155-182 ('legacy --reapply path still succeeds') verify the deprecation banner is printed — that test gets deleted. The drift-recovery path becomes `fab uninstall && fab install`.

**8. helpers/init-test-utils.ts.runInit() helper signature**: 5+ integration tests call runInit(target, {reapply, force, planOnly}). Reducing the signature requires updating all callers in lock-step.

**9. cli-surface.test.ts.snap is committed**: snapshot regeneration is part of the implementation, not test setup. Run `vitest -u` once at end of refactor.

clarification_needs.question: After --force is killed on `fab doctor` and `fab serve`, how should the lock-conflict error message guide users?
clarification_needs.context: Both doctor.ts:108 and serve.ts:61 currently pass {force: args.force} to checkLockOrThrow / acquireLock, with the user-facing message at serve-lock.ts:51,104 saying 'Stop the other process (PID N) or run with --force to override'. Without --force, the message needs to (a) drop the --force hint AND (b) decide whether to auto-suggest `kill PID` or just say 'stop the other serve process and retry'. The PID is available from the lock state.
options: Plain abort: error message says 'fab serve is running (PID N) — stop it and retry' with no kill command suggestion (safest, user decides)
Suggest kill: error message includes 'Run: kill PID' as a copy-paste hint (helpful but assumes user wants to terminate the other serve)
Auto-detect + offer: in interactive TTY, prompt 'Another serve is running (PID N). Kill it? [y/N]' — only with explicit consent
Status-only: error message includes the lock state details (PID + acquired-at timestamp) and leaves recovery to user

clarification_needs.question: After --clean-empties is killed AND becomes default behavior, should the internal cleanEmpties option in UninstallOptions / BootstrapUninstallOptions also be deleted, or kept as an internal toggle with default true?
clarification_needs.context: The cleanEmpties option threads through uninstall.ts → uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts (L24, L54, L191, L212, L234, L493, L538, L569, L604). After the CLI flag is gone, the option could (a) survive as an internal-API surface with default true (allows non-CLI callers to opt out) or (b) be deleted entirely with cleanEmpties:true hardcoded throughout. Option (b) is cleaner but loses extensibility; option (a) keeps the type surface but adds dead code paths.
options: Delete the option entirely — hardcode cleanEmpties:true at every call site
Keep the option type, default to true, no CLI flag — preserves internal API for future use
Keep cleanEmpties:true only in BootstrapUninstallOptions (the leaf helper), drop from UninstallOptions (CLI-adjacent)
Keep both option types, add a deprecation comment noting the CLI flag was removed in rc.15

clarification_needs.question: Should the rename --apply-lint → --fix-knowledge also rename internal identifiers (applyLint variable, ApplyLintPlan type, runDoctorApplyLint server function, DoctorApplyLintReport type)?
clarification_needs.context: Internal identifiers in doctor.ts (applyLint variable, ApplyLintPlan, computeApplyLintPlan, renderApplyLintPlan, renderApplyLintMutations, resolveApplyLintConsent, APPLY_LINT_CODE_LABELS) and the server-package exports (runDoctorApplyLint, DoctorApplyLintReport) all carry the apply-lint name. The spec only requires the CLI flag rename. Renaming internals provides naming consistency but expands the patch scope significantly (touches server package + tests that mock these names).
options: CLI-only rename: keep internals as applyLint/ApplyLintPlan etc. (smallest blast radius)
Full rename: cascade fix-knowledge through all internal identifiers and server-package exports (cleanest naming, large patch)
CLI flag + cli/src/commands/doctor.ts local renames; leave server package untouched (middle ground)
CLI flag + add a 'fixKnowledge' alias at the boundary; deprecate applyLint internals in a later RC

clarification_needs.question: After --reapply and --force are killed on `fab install`, should the drift-abort error message be updated to drop those flags from its guidance text?
clarification_needs.context: cli.install.diff.drift-abort (en.ts:213-214) currently advises both `fab doctor` (inspect) AND `fab uninstall && fab install` (reset). The current rc.14 deprecation warning (cli.install.diff.deprecation-force, deprecation-reapply at L215-L218) tells users these flags will be removed. In rc.15, the drift-abort message should no longer mention --force/--reapply as options (they're gone). The 'fab uninstall && fab install' guidance is the canonical recovery path.
options: Update drift-abort message: keep `fab doctor` (inspect) + `fab uninstall && fab install` (reset), drop any --force/--reapply mention
Add a new dedicated 'how to reset' help section in cli.install.diff.* and reference it from drift-abort
Keep current message — it already focuses on uninstall+install; just delete the deprecation-force/-reapply keys

clarification_needs.question: Should --rescan on doctor invoke runInitScan BEFORE or AFTER the doctor report, and should it compose with --fix-knowledge / --fix?
clarification_needs.context: doctor's new --rescan flag will call runInitScan from scan.ts:178 (same function install.ts:926 uses) to rebuild .fabric/agents.meta.json + per-file forensic state. Placement matters: BEFORE the report means the report reflects the freshly-scanned state; AFTER would make the report use stale state. Composition: --rescan is read-only-ish (rebuilds derived state), --fix and --fix-knowledge mutate user-visible state — they can probably compose, but the mutation-confirm prompt (currently inside --fix-knowledge path) would need to apply.
options: rescan runs BEFORE report; composable with --fix and --fix-knowledge (single-pass: rescan → mutations → report)
rescan runs BEFORE report; mutually exclusive with --fix-knowledge (forces user to pick: rebuild OR mutate, not both in one invocation)
rescan is a separate exit path: --rescan does rescan-only, no report rendered, no other flags allowed
rescan triggers ONLY when no other flags are set (default-doctor + --rescan composes; --fix/--fix-knowledge silently overrides)

_metadata.task_description: rc.15 Phase 2 cleanup. Kill/rename flags on fab install (force, reapply, interactive, no-bootstrap, no-mcp, no-hooks, mcp-install, scope; rename plan→dry-run), fab uninstall (force, interactive, no-bootstrap, no-mcp, no-scaffold, purge, clean-empties; rename plan→dry-run; clean-empties becomes default), fab doctor (force; rename apply-lint→fix-knowledge; add rescan), fab serve (force).
